---
name: Bug Report
about: Report a bug to help us improve RK-SAVR
title: '[Bug] '
labels: bug
assignees: ''
---

## Description

A clear and concise description of the bug.

## To Reproduce

Steps to reproduce the behavior:

1. Initialize RKSAVR with '...'
2. Call method '...'
3. Pass data '...'
4. See error

## Expected Behavior

A clear and concise description of what you expected to happen.

## Actual Behavior

What actually happened, including error messages or stack traces.

## Code Sample

If applicable, provide a minimal code sample that reproduces the issue:

```javascript
import { RKSAVR } from 'rksavr';

// Your code here
```

## Environment

- **OS**: [e.g., macOS 14.0, Ubuntu 22.04]
- **Node.js version**: [e.g., 18.17.0, 20.10.0]
- **RK-SAVR version**: [e.g., 1.0.0]
- **Package manager**: [e.g., npm 9.8.0, yarn 1.22.0]

## Data Context

If the issue is related to data processing:

- Input data characteristics (e.g., length, range, NaN values)
- Configuration used (scaleA1, scaleA2, sampleSize, etc.)
- Whether the issue occurs with synthetic or real data

## Screenshots / Plots

If applicable, add screenshots or plots to help explain the problem.

## Additional Context

Add any other context about the problem here. Include references to the paper (arXiv:2509.20015v3) if the issue relates to algorithm fidelity.

## Checklist

- [ ] I have searched existing issues to avoid duplicates
- [ ] I have tested with the latest version of RK-SAVR
- [ ] I have included a minimal reproduction case
- [ ] I have provided environment details
